feed.subtitle: SAP Build blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP & AGILITA Workshop on SAP Build: what a great experience!
entry.content:
At the end of last week I had a very stimulating trip to Berlin, where I participated in the SAP & AGILITA Workshop on SAP Build. It was an interesting event and I'm happy to share my takeaways with you.

Thursday November 27, 8:30 AM: I join my colleagues who arrived from Walldorf and Helsinki for a breakfast at our hotel, and we quickly head up to SAP Berlin office. Once there, together with the Berlin colleagues, we set up the event rooms we had booked for the keynote and then the hands-on workshop.

At 10.30, attendees start to arrive: we have the pleasure to welcome business users and developers from Berliner Verkehrsbetriebe (BVG), Bundesdruckerei, Stiftung Das Rauhe Haus, Hochschule für Technik und Wirtschaft Berlin as well as partners and a few SAP employees. All together, we engage discussions over a buffet of snacks before the event kicks off at 11.00 with our keynote, joined by many attendees on LinkedIn live and moderated by Esmeé.

Keynote and solution deep dive

Diana Roesner starts the keynote by introducing the Clean Core concept and explaining how SAP Build can be used to easily create side-by-side extensions to an SAP application. On this very important topic, I highly recommend you a new white paper - “Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud” - and a crisp and easy-to consume learning journey - “Practicing Clean Core Extensibility For SAP S/4HANA Cloud”.

We then dive into the different components of the SAP Build solution, with my close colleagues Kanishka presenting SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Code, and Yumnah describing SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Work Zone. Jesse gives a great demo of how SAP Build can help 🎅🏻 Santa Company 🎄 streamline order approvals by creating an extension directly from his SAP S/4HANA ERP using our Extensibility Wizard. Finally Hemant provides an insightful perspective on how SAP Build supports line-of-businesses, including finance, procurement, supply chain, customer experience, human resources, IT and cross-functional:

Following the presentations, our experts held a Q&A session with the audience, answering the questions raised on-site as well as in the online chat:

We received very positive feedback from the audience on this session, which you can watch as a replay.

You can also download the slides of this keynote.

Hands-on Workshop

After a short break, we moved to another room for the hands-on workshop. In 3 hours, our local participants had the opportunity to create a full-stack application to improve customer loyalty. The purpose of the application is to allow customers to redeem points from their purchases. To do this, the application needs to access customer, purchase and product data from SAP S/4HANA.

Specifically, workshop participants experienced using SAP Build Code to create a back-end CAP service with Joule AI copilot, highlighting SAP's generative AI power for development. The customer loyalty application is connected to SAP S/4HANA Cloud as an extension to the ERP, so the participants learned how to use a BTP destination. And they used SAP Build Apps to create a mobile app, showcasing our modern low-code/no-code development tool.

This is the part of the event I was involved in, and I have to say it was great and rewarding to see our participants amazed at how Joule helped them create data entities, sample data and JavaScript code from simple prompts.  They really experienced the power of Generative AI that we showcased at SAP TechEd a few weeks ago!

If you would like to do this hands-on exercise and experience Joule by yourself, it’s very easy!

Indeed, it is available here in our Basic Trial environment, where you will have immediate access to all the tools needed: SAP Build Code - Generative AI-powered development with Joule copilot.

Christmas Market at AGILITA’s

At 16:00 we jumped into a bus who drove us to the AGILITA office in Berlin. There, AGILITA Christmas Market brought everyone together to reflect and share ideas in a festive atmosphere.

Thomas Neuhaus, Head of Digital Experience for AGILITA, presented SAP AppHaus Workshops: SAP and AGILITA are helping customers find the right path to digitalization with design and architecture thinking workshops. Customers discover how to integrate Al and innovation for their own use cases, and how to effectively improve their business processes.

We also had the opportunity to attend very interesting demos of applications developed by the AGILITA team with SAP Build, showing how tasks like recording time or logging work can happen in just 3 clicks:  

AGIL AI.TimeManager  - Optimize your time and activity recording with AGIL Time Manager, the flexible and user- friendly solution for efficient time management and seamless integration in real-time.
➡️ More on AGIL AI.TimeManager in SAP Store.

 

AGIL Al.Sales  - Boost sales performance with the power of voice and Al synergy. Effortlessly dictate orders and meeting notes while on the go - advanced generative Al transcribes, analyzes, and seamlessly updates your systems in real time.
➡️ More on AGIL AI.Sales in SAP Store.

 Discover more AGILITA’s applications and extensions in SAP Store.

Up to you now!

You too can take advantage of the content we have prepared for this event. And it's very simple!

  1. Watch the keynote replay 🎞  ️
  2. Download the presentation 👍🏻  
  3. Do the hands-on exercise 👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓

 

Personal note

To end this blog post on a more personal note, I would like to say that I had 2 very energizing days in Berlin. I only work 3 days a week, Monday-Wednesday, but when I was asked if I could attend this event in Berlin on a Thursday, I didn't hesitate for a second: as I work 100% from home in Lyon, it was a great opportunity to meet up with colleagues from Berlin and Walldorf. The day after the event, we took time to reflect on what we did in 2024 and what we would like to do in 2025. This trip to Berlin was also a nice opportunity to meet great people from our valued partner AGILITA.

And when I was on my way back to Lyon on Friday evening, what caught my eye in the Berlin airport terminal?

On a big screen, an ad from SAP promoting our magical trifecta of Process + Data + AI 😊

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What' s New in Q4 2024
entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP is always evolving to better serve our customers. With the latest update in Q4 2024, in addition to quarterly content maintenance we have added new product pages.

We collaborated closely with SAP BTP experts to add three new product pages that consolidate links to all available release communication channels, like schedules, pre- and post-release communication, blogs, and more.

The new pages are:

  1. SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
  2. SAP Mobile Services
  3. SAP Translation Hub

Read more about the details in this blog post.

 

1. SAP BTP, Kyma runtime

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, documentation on releases and available modules, the GitHub Kyma repositories, as well as a helpful learning journey and hands-on tutorials.

Check it out.

Fig 1. The new page for SAP BTP, Kyma runtime.

 

2. SAP Mobile Services

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, and helpful documentation.

Check it out.

Fig 2. The new page for SAP Mobile Services.

 

3. SAP Translation Hub

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, and helpful documentation.

Check it out.

Fig 3. The new page for SAP Translation Hub.

 

Currently, the release navigator for SAP BTP covers content for 30 products and services on dedicated pages. For all products and services not featured with their own page you can find helpful resources on the new General page.

Expect more new pages dedicated to specific products and services in future updates and don't hesitate to let us know via our feedback survey, which products or services you would like to see added next. 

 

Please note that the product page for SAP Data Intelligence Cloud has been retired as of this update. For more information, you can refer to this blog post.

 

For centralized information on when SAP BTP products and services release new features refer to the Consolidated Release Schedule for SAP BTP.

Regarding Release Navigator updates, you can refer to this update plan:

*Disclaimer:  The schedule is subject to change without prior notice including, when necessary, delays or incorporating changes in subsequent update windows and not a commitment, promise, or legal obligation to deliver any material, code, or functionality.

Looking ahead, 2025 updates will mainly focus on quarterly maintenance of release resources and a steady extension of product coverage. Currently, there are no plans for further mayor UI updates in the near future.

 

The Readiness@Scale team at SAP Enterprise Support is dedicated to ensuring release readiness across the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio. Our mission is to empower SAP customers to be prepared and ready to adopt and utilize innovations.

If you have any feedback or specific requests for content in the release navigator, please don't hesitate to reach out to us.

 

And don't forget to check out our team's blogs tagged with Readiness@Scale.

Happy navigating!

Readiness@Scale 
SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Connecting Cloud ALM with BPA and configuring, triggering of automation in BPA upon job failure
entry.content:
Logon to BTP cockpit

Navigate to Overview section

Take note of Tenant ID and Subaccount ID

Connecting Cloud ALM to BPA :

Log on to SAP Cloud ALM tenant

Navigate to Administration -> Landscape Management

Click on the Service

Choose Add -> New Service

Enter relevant details as below in the window that pops up

The Root URL and Logon URL are the URL's from the lobby page of BPA. Please omit /lobby before using it in the Root URL and Logon URL.

Click Save.

Scroll to ENDPOINTS

Click Add

Next step is to obtain the Service key

Logon to SAP BTP Cockpit.  

  •  Navigate to the subaccount where SAP Build Process Automation is subscribed.
  • Select Instances and Subscriptions. 
  •  Select the service instance for SAP Build Process Automation.
  •  Select tab Service Keys. 
  •  Select Actions --> Download.

Create API Key

  •  Logon to SAP Build Process Automation. 
  •  Select Control Tower.
  •  Create an API key with scopes trigger_read and trigger_execute for the environments you want to use.
  •  Copy the generated API key.

While generating the API key activate

trigger_read

trigger_execute

Activate for all environments

Save the generated Key on a notepad

Return back to CALM screen and add endpoint

Once setup, Ping connection to test.

Congratulations. You have not successfully connected Cloud ALM with BPA.

As a next step, if a job fails in CALM then it needs to trigger the automation built in BPA. Let's configure this now!

Let's create a trigger in BPA.

Log into BPA

Select project that needs to be triggered upon job failure in CALM

Create -> Process

Click on Add a trigger -> API Trigger

Create API trigger

Add a process step

Choose Automation that needs to be triggered 

Choose the automation that needs to be triggered and Save it

Most important step is to release and deploy it. Else the trigger will not be identified by CALM

Click on Editable and deploy it.

Now let's set up CALM to trigger the automation in BPA upon job failure:

Login to CALM

Navigate to Operations -> Operation Automation

Register Operational Flow -> SAP Build Process Automation

End point gets automatically identified

Process Automation section, choose the Trigger that we built in BPA

Use Case : Job & Automation Monitoring

We now need to set up the Job and Automation Monitoring.

Operations -> Job & Automation Monitoring

Choose System

Choose the job that needs to be monitored

Click on Settings -> System

Click Events -> Add

Start Operational Flow -> Add -> Add Operational Flow

Select the Configured Trigger and Save it

Congratulation, We have now set up connectivity between SAP Cloud ALM and BPA and also configured triggering automation flows in BPA upon job failure.

entry.title: Customize with confidence on the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
In my conversations with customers, I keep hearing that extensibility is extremely important to them. It allows their systems to adapt and grow with their businesses and enables customization to fit specific needs and easy integration with other tools, enhancing overall efficiency and user experience. With SAP BTP, we are providing a stable and reliable way to keep innovating and expanding your SAP solution beyond its standard capabilities. This year at SAP TechEd, we announced the general availability of the new SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
 
The Extensibility Wizard is a tool that helps you quickly and easily add new features or customize the standard capabilities of your SAP software to better fit your business needs, without requiring extensive technical expertise.

In this blog, I’ll recap what we shared at SAP TechEd 2024 around the Extensibility Wizard, its main business value for customers, and provide some key resources to help you get started. I’ll also share my thoughts about how this release fits into SAP’s vision for extensibility and reveal a sneak peek at what lies ahead.

SAP Build Extensibility Wizard at TechEd 2024

 

 

 

 

 

The Extensibility Wizard was among this year’s key announcements at SAP TechEd. During the keynote, we also shared the news that ABAP environments are now part of SAP Build, and that customers can develop ERP extensions within a single suite of AI-driven tools. The Extensibility Wizard takes this a step further by bringing the ABAP and non-ABAP worlds together directly within the ERP, making SAP Build the default solution to modernize your core system. See all of this year’s main announcements in the TechEd keynote replay: SAP TechEd 2024 Keynote - Where ideas get real.

In case you missed it, I highly recommend checking out these on-demand sessions featuring the Extensibility Wizard, including strategy, common scenarios, demos and more:

  • AD102 | Unlock SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility with SAP Build
  • AD109 | Experience the next level of enterprise automation with SAP
  • XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio
  • IN105 | Unleash the power of AI-driven enterprise automation with SAP solutions

What’s the main business value of the Extensibility Wizard?

From SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 onwards, the wizard is now available in every business application that is enabled for key user adaptation. Both key users and developers can launch the wizard directly from their SAP S/4HANA system to start creating on-stack extensions, as well as side-by-side extensions with BTP and SAP Build. In the future, we’ll plan to enable it in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud and on-prem.

See these blogs to catch up on what’s available and how to get started:

  • SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Creating side-by-side process extensions from SAP S/4HANA Cloud 
  • The Extensibility Wizard: One Tool to Rule Them All?

 

 

 

So with the Extensibility Wizard readily available, what’s the business value customers can now achieve? Here are the main benefits to know:

  • All extensibility options brought together in one place: The Extensibility Wizard consolidates on-stack extensions that are already possible like custom field and logic, together with side-by-side – bringing BTP and S/4HANA Cloud together in a single extensibility space.
  • Contextualized experience for extension development: One of the wizard’s main capabilities is that it automatically understands the business context of the application you are using and filters the corresponding extension points, such as APIs and events.  For on-stack extensions, it navigates you to the respective business application or key user extensibility apps.
  • Leverages generative AI and other BTP innovations: By providing seamless navigation into SAP Build, now customers and partners can take advantage of the latest BTP capabilities, such as AI agents and prebuilt content, to truly accelerate their ERP modernization.

SAP Build as part of SAP’s overall vision for extensibility

With the release of Extensibility Wizard and the addition of ABAP environments to SAP Build, we are realizing our vision for more accessible extensibility and clean core development.

As the graphic below illustrates, SAP Build provides an entire suite of extensibility options to meet every type of developer persona or business need. From low- to pro-code, backend to frontend development – all of them are under the umbrella of SAP Build.

Just as SAP Build has made app development accessible to citizen developers, the Extensibility Wizard now makes extension creation a smoother and more streamlined experience that is clean core by default.

It guides key users through the first steps, making it easier to start and know which tools to use. Professional developers no longer need to start from scratch, as they can leverage features like gen AI and maintained, prebuilt content. SAP Build continues to enable fusion development from multiple angles, making it a truly holistic portfolio for extensibility.

Want to learn more about our vision for extensibility?

  • See five use cases of SAP Build extending SAP S/4HANA for finance, supply chain and other lines of business in the eBook.
  • Explore our overall strategy for clean core and extensibility model in this whitepaper.

Looking ahead to a new Extensibility era

With the Extensibility Wizard and our other TechEd milestones, we’re thrilled to take extensibility into a new era, where the worlds of BTP, S/4HANA, and ABAP come closer together! Developers and business users alike will be able to extend and innovate their core systems based on their specific skill sets and custom needs. And it’s only the beginning.

This visual illustrates how SAP Build encompasses the development of enterprise-ready extensions. You’ll note that generative AI is a centerpiece of the portfolio, with many new capabilities announced at TechEd to further accelerate the development process. AI is already changing how we interact with technology and develop software, and it’s a trend that will continue to shape the entire industry.

So, what’s next for the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard?

Currently, it’s possible to create custom fields, logic, and process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation. The option to create custom apps with SAP Build Code is on the roadmap.

We’re also pleased to announce that the Extensibility Wizard is targeted for SAP SuccessFactors in the future, offering a more robust and standardized way to enhance core HR systems. The Extensibility Wizard will operate with the same behavior and framework as it does in SAP S/4HANA, integrating SAP Build within Employee Center with numerous event hooks available.

For a sneak peek at how this will look, check out the announcement from this year’s SAP SuccessConnect event in Lisbon by Siva Sundaresan, SVP of Applications Engineering at SAP SuccessFactors. He also shares a demo of the Extensibility Wizard in the Onboarding application: Road Map and Vision Session (starts around 15:45).

***

Hopefully this got you inspired and ready to kick off your transformation journey with the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard. There are plenty more capabilities to come, so stay tuned!

For additional learning, see these Devtoberfest sessions and demos:

  • S/4HANA Extensions: Start Fast with the New Extensibility Wizard
  • What software developers want to know about Key User Extensibility
  • Demo - Easily extend SAP S/4HANA to create clean, core compliant extensions using the extensibility wizard
  • Demo - GenAI and SAP Build: Accelerate Development Projects

entry.title: Which S/4HANA extensibility option is the best for thriving as an SAP partner in the cloud space?
entry.content:
Get more value from S/4HANA with SAP Extensibility options, part 3

In my first article   of this series, I explained why extensibility options are crucial for S/4HANA customers. In the second article , I explained which option I should choose for the different use cases from a customer's point of view. In this last article, I want to focus on existing and new SAP partners who wish to connect their cloud offerings to S/4HANA customers or migrate their existing SAP ECC add-on to ABAP Cloud.

I have worked for almost 30 years in the SAP ERP space and have seen many innovations in SAP software. I started in 1996 when SAP introduced the client-server ERP and RFC libraries. During the turn of the century, SAP adopted the Internet and launched its new dimension portfolio, and third-party integration products were introduced into the market. SAP has stepped into the cloud in the last decade and fully adopted artificial intelligence.

There are a lot of changes at the core of ERP, but the challenges in my job are almost the same. As a developer, enterprise architect, SAP mentor, speaker, teacher, and advisor, I ask myself, ‘How can I add additional value for my customers out of an ERP system?’ And the answer is always the same. Focus on the missing functionality. SAP always delivers best-practice solutions that never fulfill all customers' requirements. This is still an ample opportunity for me, consultancy companies, SAP add-on solution providers, and all other software vendors.

For the last eight years, I have mostly worked for big cloud companies like Blackline, Booking, and Zuora to advise them to connect their cloud portfolio and systems to SAP S/4HANA. The cloud companies' customers are also big SAP customers. In this article, I will share my experience and tell you which S/4HANA extensibility options fit the best cloud vendors that want to thrive as SAP partners.

Challenges for SAP Solution Partners

The question is easy, but the answer is more complex. As I wrote in my previous article, SAP supports several S/4HANA extensibility options, which depend on the S/4HANA releases and versions. So you need to answer the question: Do you want to support the S/4HANA Cloud private version, the S/4HANA Cloud public version, or even support ‘classic’ SAP ECC?  But also, do you want to build a standalone add-on that runs on Business Technology Platform Software as a Service (BTP SAAS) or is embedded on S/4HANA? Or do you want to connect your partner cloud solution to S/4HANA?

But there are more answers needed. The data and events must be publicly available in the S/4HANA system. How will we handle the data exchange's latency, performance, size, and security? Who will be responsible when something goes wrong, technical, infra, and business-wise? Will the current SAP license be enough, or will there be additional operational and license costs for my customers? How will user management, authentication, authorization, and security be handled? How will the execution be monitored, and how will alerting and mapping be arranged? How should sensitive data be managed, and what audit trails and data should be archived? How will networking, security, risk, and complaint departments react to the solution? Will the used platform and tooling be future-proof? And so we can ask a lot more questions.

Ultimately, all these questions should be answered to develop the best solution. However, in my experience, not all SAP partners consider these questions.

SAP positions the S/4HANA Cloud public edition as the preferred version to GROW with SAP in the long term. However, SAP also provides the S/4HANA Cloud private edition for existing customers to RISE, with SAP as the golden path to move to the S/4HANA Cloud public edition in the future.

We will first focus on the S/4HANA Cloud public edition because this release is SAP’s long-term solution. Next, we can consider other SAP ERP systems, such as the S/4HANA Cloud private edition, S/4HANA on-premise, and SAP ECC.

S/4HANA extensibility options

In my second article, I described the eight extensibility options for S/4HANA. However, if we look at partner solutions, we can skip the classic extensibilities because they are not supported in the public edition and are obsolete from a certification point of view. Also, the low-code extensibilities options S/4HANA Key User Extensibility, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Build Apps could be more suitable because they mainly focus on custom developments for the citizen developer.

Figure 1 – Possible Partner Integration Options

 

This limited the possible extensibility options, and because we can also access the S/4HANA APIs and events directly, we will have five possibilities (See Figure 1) to deliver a partner solution:

  • An add-on application on the S/4HANA embedded ABAP environment
  • A partner application as a SAAS application running on SAP BTP ABAP environment
  • A partner application as a SAAS application developed with SAP Build Code on SAP BTP
  • An integration package on SAP Cloud Integration
  • Running your application on a non-SAP solution and directly accessing S/4HANA events and APIs.

Using APIs and events

Software vendors that run their solutions on their cloud will primarily consider the last option and try to connect their cloud solution directly to S/4HANA events and APIs. But from my experience, this isn’t the right approach. The S/4HANA Cloud public edition strongly depends on business events, and most cloud vendor solutions aren’t prepared for this. Due to the configuration possibilities in the S/4HANA Cloud and the use of APIs, the responsibility for root cause analysis shifts from the customer to the cloud vendor, which needs to build a big SAP knowledge support center. Also, the cloud vendor should handle all security, risk, and compliance issues, which are initially the customer's responsibility.

Another issue with this approach is that you, as a partner, cannot certify the solution with SAP. SAP is not responsible for the solution's availability and performance, code changes, or deprecation of APIs and events. This approach carries a high risk for customers and the cloud vendor.

SAP Cloud Integration

A better approach is an integration package that can be installed on the customer's SAP Cloud Integration system. In this case, SAP will ensure smooth integration between S/4HANA and SAP Cloud Integration. The solution can be certified, and as a cloud vendor, you can mainly focus on integrating your cloud partner solution and SAP Cloud integration. The downside of this approach is that SAP Cloud Integration is separate from any S/4HANA Cloud edition license and should be brought by the customer separately. Only some customers are willing to do so. Most customers already have an existing integration product for more than just their SAP landscape. The need for separate SAP Cloud Integration licenses and setting up a support and knowledge center to maintain SAP Cloud Integration will be a blocker. Providing an integration package as a partner solution will limit the number of SAP customers you can connect to your cloud solution in the market.

ABAP Cloud environments

The best approach for cloud vendors is to build the connectivity as an add-on product on S/4HANA, a SAAS solution on BTP, or a combination of these options. Building your partner integration solution on an ABAP Cloud environment is the best extensibility option for applications strongly dependent on S/4HANA. Based on my investigation, cloud vendors will profit the most from this option. They can shift the responsibility of the availability and maintenance of the landscape to SAP; they are highly flexible in a robust and stable environment following SAP’s future path and can directly profit from the innovations SAP is launching. The barriers to developing these extensions are higher, but the barriers for customers will be lower. There will be no additional costs for running the developer extensions on the embedded ABAP cloud environment in S/4HANA Cloud. When cloud vendors run their solution as a SAAS solution on BTP, they can share the costs with multiple customers. The advantage of the SAAS solution above the Addon is that the cloud vendor fully controls the code line, and the customer is still responsible for accessing and connecting to their S/4HANA tenant.

Another significant advantage of building connectivity from S/4HANA to your cloud solution on ABAP Cloud for BTP as a SAAS solution is that you can connect to the S/4HANA cloud public edition, make your future-proof solution with one code line, and downport the connection to other ERP versions, such as the S/4HANA cloud private edition, S/4HANA on-premise, or even SAP ECC.

Figure 2 – Codeline options of ABAP Cloud

 

Because the ABAP Cloud on the BTP code line follows SAP’s Clean core strategy, it should also run as an add-on on the S/4HANA Cloud editions when these editions support the same underlying ABAP Cloud version.

As mentioned before, in SAP ECC, many SAP solution partners have already provided an add-on to solve the missing functionality of standard SAP. However, this add-on should be rewritten to be S/4HANA Cloud compliant because SAP only accepts code that follows SAP’s Clean core strategy. The SAP solution partner can rewrite the code as S/4HANA Cloud Developer Extensibility, but in this case, the partner always depends on the customers' upgrade strategy. Suppose he wants to control the versioning of his code line and innovations. In that case, the partner can rewrite the addon as a side-by-side extensibility with SAP Build Code in SAP CAP or ABAP, which connects to S/4HANA. Or he can even run the applications in a non-SAP cloud environment of his choice and follow the extensibility options described above. From my experience, all these options are valid, future-proof, and in line with the solution for cloud vendors.

Cloud and SAP Add-on vendors who want to build their solutions with ABAP Cloud can even speed up their development, shorten the time to market, and shift responsibilities back to their customers using an OEM solution like the FEXS API Connect from fexs.com. These OEM solutions already take care of the most common tasks, resulting in less development needed, smaller SAP support departments, and higher customer satisfaction.

Conclusion

Because SAP and its customers are still on the move with S/4HANA to its future Cloud ERP landscape, it isn’t easy for SAP Solution partners to make the right decision for their future-proof solution.  The move of SAP to the cloud and the openness and flexibility of its portfolio will give SAP Addon partners and existing cloud solution providers an excellent opportunity to bring added value to the existing SAP community.  Suppose your solution strongly depends on S/4HANA processes. In that case, I believe a partner application running as S/4HANA Cloud Developer Extensibility or on a SAP BTP ABAP Environment following SAP Clean Core strategy is the best and most future-proof solution. An article is always too short to explain all the details, but I hope this article triggers your mind.  And in my job as a strategic architect and advisor, I’m open to any discussion and available to help push you in the right direction.

This article was initially posted on the linked-in profile of Robert Eijpe

entry.title: Joule is Now Integrated into SAP Build Work Zone!
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce that Joule, SAP’s generative AI assistant, is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, SAP Start, and SAP Mobile Start. This marks a significant step in empowering users to work faster and smarter, with seamless access to insights and information at their fingertips.

Integrating Joule into SAP Build Work Zone brings a unique value to users by effortlessly connecting data from multiple backend systems into a single interface. By providing answers and insights from any supported system, Joule eliminates the need to switch between tools. This empowers users to stay informed with all relevant information and applications in one place.

What is Joule? 

Joule is SAP’s cutting-edge generative AI assistant, designed to bring intelligence and efficiency to every aspect of work. Joule helps users can interact naturally, ask questions, and get precise answers related to their business processes. For instance, end users can inquire about the status of a purchase order and be guided directly to it, streamlining their workflow and saving valuable time.

Joule isn’t just a productivity tool; it’s a game-changer for decision making and operational efficiency. It makes everyday tasks easier and more intuitive for end users.

 Very recently, Joule turned 1! Learn more about Joule here.

BIG NEWS: Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

With our latest release, Joule is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. This enables end users to interact with Joule and get answers from all connected systems​.

This boosts productivity by leveraging Joule intuitive capabilities with SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. Users can navigate quickly to the functionality needed for their next task, receive precise answers on how to use SAP applications, and complete tasks effortlessly using natural language requests. It can help navigate to relevant applications and perform transactions. Additionally, Joule empowers more informed decision making with answers derived directly from your business document.

Joule can also serve as your personal guide within SAP Build Work Zone, helping you navigate and make the most of your site. Whether you need assistance with managing content or accessing specific features, Joule provides step-by-step guidance.

Here is an example of how you can use Joule within your Work Zone site: Imagine you're trying to track the status of a purchase order. Instead of navigating through multiple screens, you simply ask Joule, “Has my purchase order been approved?”. Joule instantly provides the status and key details directly within its window in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. This seamless interaction eliminates the need to switch between applications, saving time and effort.

Prerequisites for Using Joule in SAP Build Work Zone

To begin leveraging Joule in your SAP Build Work Zone environment, ensure the following prerequisites are met:

  • Joule requires Identity Authentication for user login. If you haven’t already switched to SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication, this step must be completed beforehand. For further guidance, see Switching to SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication.
  • Before completing the Joule onboarding tasks, it should be ensured that he required prerequisites for both SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and for Joule have been completed. 
  • Run the Joule booster to activate the integration and complete the setup process as seen in the steps below.

Set up subaccount by selecting the subaccount that should be used for Joule provisioning and configurations. 

Selecting the SAP solutions where Joule should be enabled.

Selecting the SAP solutions whose capability packages should be provisioned in Joule.

How to Get Started with the Integration

Integrating Joule into your SAP Build Work Zone is straightforward. After successfully running the Joule booster and completing the integration between SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule, a new setting becomes available in the Site Setting screen of your site.

As an administrator, navigate to the Site Settings screen and enable the Joule option under the Services section. Once enabled, the Joule icon will appear in the header of your runtime site, giving users instant access to the AI-powered assistant for enhanced productivity and elevated outcomes across business.

Please note that Joule is not available in SAP BTP trial account. 

Looking Ahead: What’s Next?

The integration of Joule into SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition is on the horizon and is coming in 2025. It will bring the Gen AI-powered experiences to an even broader set of users. The advanced edition will offer a wider scope with additional capabilities, tailored to meet diverse business needs. This expansion ensures that every end user can fully harness generative AI for greater efficiency and smarter decision-making.

Joule’s integration into SAP Build Work Zone is more than an enhancement—it's a transformation. It combines the power of SAP Build Work Zone with Joule's intelligence, letting users focus on what matters most. While the complexities of business processes are handled by a trusted AI assistant.

Stay tuned for latest updates as we bring even more innovation to your SAP Build experience.

***

Learn more:

➤ Announcement blog for Joule in SAP Mobile Start

➤ SAP Build Work Zone on: sap.com / SAP Community

➤ SAP Builders Community Group

➤ More information on new product features: Roadmap

entry.title: Extending SAPUI5 apps: Adaptation projects vs. Extension projects
entry.content:
SAPUI5 flexibility to adapt standard SAP apps

SAPUI5 is SAP’s strategic Web framework for Web applications such as SAP Fiori. In this context, SAPUI5 flexibility offers capabilities to adapt standard apps, not only for developers but also for key users and end users using simple WYSIWYG drag and drop tools. Learn about all the capabilities: SAPUI5 Flexibility - All You Need to Know. SAPUI5 flexibility provides features to realize a clean core approach with on-stack extensions: SAP standard apps remain untouched, no modifications are needed, and even predefined extension points are unnecessary.


SAPUI5 flexibility - Developer adaptation

Developers can leverage the concepts of SAPUI5 flexibility by using SAPUI5 adaptation projects in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), the development environment of SAP Build Code. Those projects contain all code-based custom adaptations, created in the new Adaptation Editor and other code editors of BAS.

Picture: The new Adaptation Editor in SAP Business Application Studio


Extension project vs. Adaptation project

While adaptation projects are the strategic, advanced technology for code-based extensions of SAPUI5 apps, we also have the predecessor extension projects. To address frequent confusions, I want to summarize the differences to help understanding what you see in your system:

 Extension projectAdaptation project
SAP strategy  Legacy usageStrategic, advanced approach, leveraging SAPUI5 flexibility concepts
Extendonly legacy apps not (yet) compliant to SAPUI5 adaptation projects (1.)most apps, as given in SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library -> Implementation Information -> Extensibility via SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects. (2.)
PrerequisitesIndividual, predefined extension points in each appGeneral enablement of app (1.)
Supported applicationsFreestyle SAPUI5 apps onlyAny SAPUI5 app (freestyle SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori elements apps)
Extension optionsLimited options (e.g. no extension of lifecycle methods)Increased options for all essential app artefacts
Extension locationExtend in predefined extension points onlyExtend anywhere (3.) (unless blocked by application logic)
Stability with upgradesLimited stabilityImproved stability
Future-proof conceptOne layer of changesMultiple layers possible (planned)
Platform supportSAP S/4HANA on-prem onlyAny platform (4.)

Footnotes

  1. Details on prerequisites for respective platforms in Extending SAP Fiori Applications.
  2. If an adaptation project is being created for an app not compliant to SAPUI5 adaptation projects, the wizard in SAP Business Application Studio automatically creates an extension project. (An example for such an app is My Inbox.)
  3. including existing extension points, controller hooks
  4. See details in Features and Availability.

What does this mean for existing extension projects?

If you have an existing extension project, you can continue using it, i.e. running it and also maintaining it. In case a) you are planning to add new extensions due to bigger requirements and b) the underlying standard app supports SAPUI5 adaptation projects (see above), SAP recommends to manually migrate the extension project to an adaptation project to make use of its advanced options. (The UI5 team currently plans to provide guidance for this manual migration.)

Further references

  • Migrate from SAP Web IDE to SAP Business Application Studio: Blog post & Mission
  • SAP Fiori Elements: Adaptation Project – Adding a Custom Filter to the List Report
  • Adaptation Project: Freestyle SAPUI5 App – Adding a Search Link to Items on a Table
  • Work with SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Santa builds process to distribute Christmas presents
entry.content:
No one would deny that Santa has a complex process to give out presents for Christmas. 

But several key new SAP Build Process Automation features has enabled Santa to automate the process using a true Fusion Team of reindeer, elves, and his wife. Among the features:

  • Scheduled triggers
  • Script task
  • Custom variables
  • Subprocesses
  • Go to task

And the process can actually teach us some of the capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation.

Scheduled Triggers

Probably the most obvious feature to help Santa is the scheduled trigger for regular processes. We could rely on one of the elves to send an API call, but the scheduled trigger is much more reliable.

Action and Data Type to Get Children 

The process uses an action to retrieves a list of children.

We first created a data type to represent a child (name string and a Boolean on whether they were nice or naughty).

Then we created custom variables, one to hold the full list of children, and another to hold the current child in our iteration.

Then we run an action to get the children.

Script Task and GoTo for Iterating Children

Now we create a script task to move the last child into the current child variable, and to remove that child from the full list.

And finally we create an iteration sequence by a Go To back to the start, where we have a condition to see if the list has more children, and 

Subprocesses

We create subprocesses like "Prepare Reindeer and Sled" since we are likely to need to do that during the year, and we create subprocesses like "Deliver via Chimney" in order to simplify and isolate our logic.

Obvious, in a more realistic case we would have created additional logic to review all the actions of a child to determine if they were nice or naughty, as Santa "sees you when you're sleepin'" and "knows when you're awake", and likely is aware of all actions of a children, so we might use a Decision to make that determination.

And we might create an automation to deal with the complexity of building the toys, handling emails in Outlook from kids wanting presents, and looking for sales on toys on the web. 

🎅🎄❄🤶⛪🕎🕌🤗🦌

Hoping the holiday season and the new year brings you peace and love and happiness, and everything you dream about.

entry.title: Santa's Early Gift for SAP BTP
entry.content:
At this time of year, as many of us are looking forward to some fun over the festive period, I like many people am busy wrapping presents for friends and family. But in the fine spirit of giving, the rather nice team at G2, playing the role of Santa in their smart red and white livery, have given the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) team an early Christmas gift, in the form of Winter 2025 badges, celebrating SAP BTP’s recognition as a Leader across a number of different categories in the G2 Winter 2025 reports. This includes a Momentum badge this time around too, indicating that our customers place us in the top 25% of leaders in certain product categories. What a nice surprise for the team, and a great way to round out 2024!

The recognition in new G2 reports builds on that received just a few short months ago in their Fall 2024 reports, and shows a continued appreciation from SAP’s customers for the value they derive from using SAP BTP, as badges are offered only following a robust scoring methodology which ranks vendor solutions based on real, verified, and trusted software buyers’ reviews. This of course gives the SAP BTP team a great feeling of pride, and gratitude too, knowing that our customers see us in this way. And, I know I express this for the whole team, when I say “thank you” to the SAP customers who left reviews with G2 that enabled us to achieve this recognition.

Where are customers feeling the value from SAP BTP?

Of course, SAP customers use SAP BTP for a variety of business needs, and so the value they feel from the platform and services, flows to them in a number of different ways. Some of this value is described in the reviews that customers leave at the SAP BTP landing page on the G2 website. Here are a few examples:

“It effortlessly connects different systems and makes everything work together smoothly. It also gives you the tools to get creative, build what you need and adapt as your business grows…flexibility for implementation is superb and the support from SAP makes it better because of which it’s very easy and convenient.”

(User review by a mid-market customer, Dec 2024)

 

“The feature I appreciate the most in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) is its multifunctionality and integration. It is possible to optimize business processes through a single platform which integrates several SAP and third-party systems together. Thanks to the cloud-based business services offered on the Platform, along with powerful analytics and ML capabilities, it is an excellent environment for building and releasing out-of-the-box solutions. And finally, the low-code/no-code environment allows business users themselves to create the required solutions and thus speed up digital change.”

(User review by small business customer, Sept 2024)

 

“SAP business technology is very User friendly & is an end to end solution…It basically solved all my procurement transformation needs.”

(User review by enterprise customer, Dec 2024)

So, as 2024 draws to a close, I’d like to thank both G2 for recognizing SAP BTP as a Leader in many of the 2024 reports as well as the new Winter 2025 reports, and of course thanks to our customers too, for all of the continued kind words and support, and for choosing SAP BTP as the platform to help run your business.

Thanks for a super end to 2024, and here’s to a wonderful 2025 ahead!

Chris Grundy, Product Marketing, SAP BTP

entry.title: Wrapping up 2024 for Application Development, Automation and Integration on SAP BTP: What a year!
entry.content:
2024 has been an incredibly busy year for our application development, automation and integration portfolio on the SAP Business Technology Platform. As the year draws to a close, I wanted to take some time to reflect on our achievements. Indeed, I am very grateful to the people in my teams who have worked very hard to deliver so many improvements to our solutions, and I would like to say a big thank you to all of them.

Of course, you've heard a lot about Joule, our AI co-pilot, over the course of the year. In fact, we have been integrating AI in general, and Generative AI with Joule in particular, into all components of our portfolio, as we announced earlier. And we demonstrated our AI capabilities at SAP TechEd two months ago. This deployment of Business AI has generated great interest from our customers and partners, as well as analysts.

But we've also introduced many new features and enhancements, which I'm proud to present in detail below.

SAP Build

In terms of application development and automation, in addition to embedding AI in all of our solutions, our biggest improvement in 2024 was to enrich SAP Build with pro-code capabilities. Indeed, we announced SAP Build Code at SAP TechEd 2023, made available in March this year, and we recently added ABAP Environments to SAP Build. SAP Build now allows business users and developers to work across their low-code, Java/JavaScript and ABAP projects from a unique starting point, the SAP Build lobby, benefitting of seamless interoperability capabilities fostering fusion development.

With this, SAP Build is now THE extensibility solution for any SAP customer: you can now accelerate your ERP extension development and modernization, foster innovation, and automate processes, all in a single, comprehensive suite of AI-driven tools for extension development. And SAP S/4HANA users can create extensions with SAP Build directly from the ERP business application, thanks to our new Extensibility Wizard for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This year, we have worked hard to improve the integration of the SAP Build components, both with other components of SAP BTP and with other SAP solutions: for example, SAP Build Process Automation is now integrated with SAP Build Apps and also with Advanced Event Mesh, applications created with SAP Build Apps can be deployed in SAP Build Work Zone, and SAP Build Work Zone supports multi-tenancy.

Finally, I would like to mention that we have improved the usability of SAP Build, for example with a new process layout and script task editor in SAP Build Process Automation and guided experiences in SAP Build Work Zone.

This image summarizes all the improvements we delivered this year:

A few weeks ago, Gartner has recognized SAP as a Visionary in its 2024 Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms (LCAP). I would like to thank the SAP Build team for achieving this great result!

You can find more details in these blog posts from our product experts:

  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – November 2024
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – October 2024
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – September 2024
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – July 2024
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Q2 2024 Edition
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Seamless Integration of Processes and Apps
  • What’s New with SAP Build Process Automation: January 2024

SAP Integration Suite

In 2024 we have also improved SAP Integration Suite in many different areas:

Regarding SAP PI/PO migration, which is a topic on which I published a blog post earlier this year to explain Advanced Event Mesh and Edge Integration Cell, we introduced a Pipeline concept and new rules added for migration assessment.

Edge Integration Cell is now available on Google Cloud and Red Hat OpenShift. It supports outgoing connections through a HTTP proxy and provides offline availability during periods of temporary connectivity loss.

This year, Event Mesh became a capability of SAP Integration Suite, which also allows direct consumption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Events and distributed tracing, from sending to receiving applications across one or multiple event brokers.

We have been investing heavily in improving developer efficiency and operational excellence. A few of Gen AI Increments that we recently launched were to accelerate development of integration flows with Gen AI and analyze APIs using API Anomaly detection in API Management

You can also accelerate connectivity with SAP Integration Suite using new adapters for Coupa, NetSuiteERP, SnowFlake, and Amazon DynamoDB, SMB, Jira, Amazon EventBridge, HubSpot, Anaplan with many more planned to be released soon.

We enhanced Cloud Integration and API Management user experience with SAP Horizon theme. Enriched the Cloud Integration capability with the ability to inspect content size for all the integration artifacts, manage access at the package level, validate integration flow design as per the guidelines in integration flow editor.

Furthermore, API Management now supports multiple target endpoints in API proxy tooling and recently launched Developer Hub for search, discovery and consumption of APIs. We also launched oData provisioning as a new capability.

We have onboarded 25+ partners with a contract and further enriched our marketplace with 53+ new partner content in particular 7 new APIs, 4 Processes, 20+ Integration packages and 22+ Validated partner use cases added on SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Finally, B2B Scenarios for Trading Partner Management (TPM) and Integration Advisor have also been enriched with functionalities like archiving of Payload data for Trading Partner Management scenarios, EDI flat file payloads can be directly used to simulate mappings, for easier access to envelope fields these can be added in the Integration Advisor, simplified mass-maintenance in TPM, support for custom SOA messages in Integration Advisor, new Tradacoms message standard.

You can find more details in these blog posts from our product experts:

  • Whats New For SAP Integration Suite – August 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – June & July 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – Spring 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – February 2024
  • What's New for SAP Integration Suite - January 2024

Feel free to follow our #WhatsNewInSAPIntegrationSuite on SAP Community and register for our webinar for updates on upcoming innovations!

Finally, I’m also proud to share that with SAP Integration Suite SAP has been named a Leader for 4th consecutive time by Gartner® in its 2024 Magic Quadrant™ for iPaaS. In addition, SAP is a Leader in IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Business-to-Business Middleware 2024 Vendor Assessment.

Kudos to the team for these achievements!

What’s for 2025?

As you’ve seen above, we delivered a lot of new features and enhancements in 2024. And for sure we will continue in 2025!

You can find out what we have planned for next year on our public roadmap for application development, automation and integration on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

With that, I wish you a Merry Christmas and very happy holidays!  🎅🏻🎄

entry.title: How can I prepare for the Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build certification
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build learning journey.

Overview
As a low-code/no-code developer, you can create advanced applications and SAP extensions with SAP Build Apps. You also have the capability to develop automations and processes using SAP Build Process Automation, integrating them with other applications through APIs. Additionally, you can design business sites and integrate applications within SAP Build Work Zone to enhance user engagement and streamline business processes.
Successfully completing this exercise confirms your comprehensive understanding and skills necessary to pursue the SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build certification as the next step.

Learning objectives

  • Interplay of SAP Build Products (SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Work Zone)
  • Confirm your proficiency and readiness for pursuing the SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build certification.

Prerequisites
To prepare for this exercise, it is necessary to take the following learning journeys

  • Developing Apps with SAP Build Apps using Drag-and-Drop Simplicity,
  • Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation
  • Designing SAP Build Work Zone Learning Journeys. 

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP Build Apps - upload files to SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX) Service with camera
entry.content:
Introduction

Uploading files is a common requirement in various business scenarios. As SAP Build Apps developer, we are often required to add file upload capability into our apps to fulfill business requirements and enhance user experience.

One of such use case is to upload documents to SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX) Service to process large business documents that have content in headers and tables. You can use the extracted information, for example, to automatically process payables, invoices, or payment notes and make sure that invoices and payables match.

Its Upload Document API requires you to sent a POST request with "multipart/form-data" payload.

In this blog, I will show how you can make use of the new "HTTP Destination Request" flow function along with "Take photo" flow function to achieve that.

By the end of this blog, you will learn how to:

  1. Set up your DOX service destination to work with Build Apps.
  2. Install your destination to be used in Build Apps and test the connection to your destination in Build Apps.
  3. Use "Take photo" to let user to capture photo with device and pass the resulting image to "HTTP destination request"
  4. Use "HTTP destination request" to upload the image to your DOX service API via the installed destination.

Pre-requisites

  1. You must have SAP Build Apps already subscribed in your subaccount.
  2. You must have SAP Document Information Extraction Service subscribed.
  3. All required roles for SAP Builds Apps and Document Information Extraction Service are assigned accordingly to your user.

Setting up Destination for Document Information Extraction Service

If you already have a working destination to your DOX service, you can use it or clone it while referring to the steps below to make it compatible with the examples in this blog:

  1. Go to SAP BTP cockpit and create a destination pointing to your DOX instance.
    • For this example, I recommend that the URL of the destination is pointing to the root of your DOX service instance:
      • Example: https://aiservices-dox.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/document-information-extraction/v1/
      • We will append additional relative path to your destination later when using the "HTTP Destination Request" flow function.
    • For authentication related configurations, you can refers to your DOX instance's service key file. These configuration are inside the "uaa" section of the file.
      • TIPS: Token Service URL typically ends with /oauth/token. The UAA URL you copied from the service key usually doesn't contain that. So, you must append /oauth/token yourself when entering the URL to the Token Service URL field.
    • Add the required additional properties in the destination for Build Apps' usage:
      • HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
      • BuildApps.Enabled: true
      • BuildApps.ApiType: rest
      • MobileEnabled: true
    • These are the minimal properties required for Build Apps, you may add other properties for other purposes too. Example:

      Hint: "Check Connection" in the BTP Destination cockpit only tells you that the URL is reachable, it does not indicate that the authentication is set up correctly. So, you should not rely solely on it to tell you that your destination is configured correctly.

Install DOX Service Destination in Your Build Apps Project

  1. Open your Build Apps project.
  2. Go to “Integrations” tab.
    1. Press on “Add Integration” button.
    2. Press on the “BTP DESTINATIONS” button in the SAP Systems card.
    3. Search for your DOX destination and select it. It should show a screen with “The destination was recognised as REST api” message.

      Note:
      • If you can't find your destination, do check that you have added the BuildApps.Enabled=true property in your destination.
      • If you don't see that message,do check that you have added the BuildApps.ApiType=rest property in your destination.
    4. Press "INSTALL INTEGRATION" button to install the destination.
    5. (Optional) Testing the connection to destination using Entity's list operation
      1. You can test the connection to your DOX destination by creating adding a REST API data entity and use it to call one of the GET API available in your service, assuming you have the right access. Otherwise, you can skip this steps or choose different API to test with.
      2. Press on the "Add REST API Data Entity" button. You should get a dialog pop up.
      3. In the dialog, enter the following information:
        • Name: Documents
        • Autogenerate configuration: enabled
        • Entity path within the API: /document/jobs
        • Enable Capabilities: enable "list" and uncheck the rest of them
        • Press "Add" button
      4. You should now see the DocumentJobs entity and its Fields and more.
        If you are seeing error here and it couldn't generate any Fields for you, then it's likely there's something wrong with your path, or destination configuration. Make sure to check your destination's URL and authentication configuration.
      5. You can use "Browse Real Data" button to check if you can see expected data entries and no errors.

      6. If you are not seeing any errors, it means the connection to your destination is good for usage.
    6. You can now Exit this page.

Using HTTP Destination Request to Upload File

  1. Go to “User Interface” tab.
  2. Press on the button (or any other UI control) that you wish to use to trigger the file upload and then press on the "Add logic to <your control>" at the bottom of the screen. This should show the Logic Canvas.
  3. Press the “Marketplace” button under the "Core" tab on the left panel.
    1. Search for “HTTP destination request” flow function and install it.
    2. After it is installed you should see it in the "INSTALLED" tab of the Logic Canvas.
  4. You are now ready to use “HTTP destination request” to upload file:
    1. From the "CORE" tab, scroll down to the "Device" category and drag “Take Photos” flow function to the logic flow canvas.
      ℹ️ Note: "Take photo" flow function is currently only available when you run your app in mobile device. It will do nothing when triggered as web app.
    2. Link the button's “Event: Component tap” node to the “Take photo” node.
    3. Go to the "INSTALLED" tab and drag “HTTP destination request” to the logic flow canvas.
    4. Link “Take photo” node’s output port 1 to the “HTTP destination request” node, because we want to pass the photo taken by user to the “HTTP destination request” node.
    5. Click on the “HTTP destination request” node and on its property panel.
      1. In the Destination dropdown select your installed destination.
      2. Set the “HTTP method” as “POST”.
      3. Set the “Request body type” as “multipart/form-data”.
      4. Set the “Request body” and add entries for each part needed for the target API. In this case, we will be using the SAP Document Information Extraction Upload Document API which requires 2 properties:
        KeyValue
        optionsBind this value to formula using the following sample: ENCODE_JSON({"schemaName":"SAP_invoice_schema","clientId": "default","documentType":"invoice","receivedDate":"2024-12-01"})

        Note: In real use cases, you can use variable to store these information instead, but you must always use ENCODE_JSON to convert them to JSON encoded text
        file

        Bind this value to formula using the following sample: MERGE([outputs["Take photo"].photoFile,{"mimeType":"image/jpeg"}])

        You will see some errors in the formula editor about unassignable object, you can safely ignore that.

        Note: This formula is needed due to an existing issue with missing mime-type in the Take photo output. Once the issue is fixed in the near future, you can simply bind this value to "Output of another node" > "Take photo / Photo file".


        Value for "options" key:

        Value for "file" key:

        Resulting list of key-values for the "Request body":
      5. Expand the Optional Inputs section of the "HTTP destination request":
        In "Path" set "/document/jobs" because that's the path to the API for uploading document to start an information extraction job. The path specified here will be appended to your destination in the final request.
      6. Your "HTTP destination request" is now ready.
    6. Drag and drop 1 "Toast" flow function to the Logic Canvas and link them to each of the output port 1 of the “HTTP destination request” flow function.
      For Toast linked to port 1 you can bind its message to formula with the following:
      "Result: " + outputs["HTTP destination request"].successStatusText
    7. Drag and drop 2 "Alert" flow function to the Logic Canvas and link them to each of the output ports of the “HTTP destination request” flow function.
      1. For the Alert linked to port 2, you can bind its message to formula with the following:
        "Error from backend: " + outputs["HTTP destination request"].failureStatusText
      2. For the Alert linked to port 3, you can bind its message to formula with the following:
        "Error: " + outputs["HTTP destination request"].error
    8. The logic canvas should looks like this:
    9. Save your project and you are ready to preview your app and test the file upload.

Preview and Trigger File Upload

"Take photo" currently only works in iOS or Android platform, therefore we will do the preview on your mobile devices.

  • On your mobile device, install the "SAP Build Apps Preview" app from Apple App Store or Google Play Store.
  • After the app is installed, launch the app on your mobile device and press the center card to create a login PIN code. You should see a temporary PIN code generated.
  • Go back to your Build Apps project in the browser and press on the "Preview" button
  • In the "Preview on your device" section, enter your temporary PIN code into the text box and press "Confirm pin". You should get "Mobile preview authenticated" message if it works.
  • Go back to the"SAP Build Apps Preview" app on your mobile device, you should see list of your apps that can be previewed.
  • Locate and open your app.
  • When the app is loaded, press the file upload button:
  • That should prompt you for permission to take photo (if this is the first time), approve it and take a photo of an invoice.
  • Once the photo is taken the upload via "HTTP destination request" should happen and you should get a result soon.
  • After successful upload you should get "Result: Created" toast:
  • If you check the Document Information Extraction web app, you should see your photo is now uploaded and queued for extraction.
  • Once the extraction is completed you should see be able to see it's result.

Summary

As summary, in this blog, you have learned how to set up your Build Apps project to make use of the "HTTP destination request" and "Take photo" to upload file using multipart/form-data payload.

If you are interested with uploading file to other services such as SAP Document Management Service using "Pick files" and "HTTP destination request" in SAP Build Apps, you can visit this blog: SAP Build Apps - upload files to SAP Document Management Service (DMS) repository with file picker

entry.title: SAP Build Apps - upload files to SAP Document Management Service (DMS) repository with file picker
entry.content:
Introduction

Uploading files is a common requirement in various business scenarios. As SAP Build Apps developer, we are often required to add file upload capability into our apps to fulfill business requirements and enhance user experience.

One of such use case is to upload documents to SAP Document Management Service (DMS) for storage purposes.

DMS Create Document API requires you to sent a POST request with "multipart/form-data" payload.

In this blog, I will show how you can make use of the new "HTTP Destination Request" flow function to achieve that in your SAP Build Apps project.

By the end of this blog, you will learn how to:

  1. Set up your DMS repository destination to work with Build Apps.
  2. Install your destination to be used in Build Apps and test the connection to your destination in Build Apps.
  3. Use "Take photo" to let user to capture photo with device and pass the resulting image to "HTTP destination request"
  4. Use "HTTP destination request" to upload the image to your DMS repository via the installed destination.

Pre-requisites

  1. You must have SAP Build Apps already subscribed in your subaccount.
  2. You must have SAP Document Management Service (DMS) subscribed with access to their API service.
  3. All required roles for SAP Builds Apps and Document Management Service are assigned accordingly to your user.

Setting up Destination for Document Management Service

If you already have a working destination to your DMS service, you can use it or clone it while referring to the steps below to make it compatible with the examples in this blog:

  1. Created a destination in SAP BTP pointing to your DMS instance.
    • For this example, I recommend that the URL of the destination is pointing to the /root API of your DMS instance:
      • Example: https://api-sdm-xyz.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/browser/1234abcd-56ef-78ab-90cd-1234abcd567/root
      • You can always append additional relative path to your destination later when using the "HTTP Destination Request" flow function.
    • Add the required additional properties in the destination for Build Apps' usage:
      • HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
      • BuildApps.Enabled: true
      • BuildApps.ApiType: rest
      • MobileEnabled: true
    • These are the minimal properties required for Build Apps, you may add other properties for other purposes too. For example:

      Hint: "Check Connection" in the BTP Destination cockpit only tells you that the URL is reachable, it does not indicate that the authentication is set up correctly. So, you should not rely solely on it to tell you that your destination is configured correctly.

Installing DMS Destination in Your Build Apps Project

  1. Open your Build Apps project.
  2. Go to “Integrations” tab.
    1. Press on “Add Integration” button.
    2. Press on the “BTP DESTINATIONS” button in the SAP Systems card.
    3. Search for your DMS repository destination and select it. It should show a screen with “The destination was recognised as REST api” message.

      Note:
      • If you can't find your destination, do check that you have added the BuildApps.Enabled=true property in your destination.
      • If you don't see that message,do check that you have added the BuildApps.ApiType=rest property in your destination.
    4. Press "INSTALL INTEGRATION" button to install the destination.
    5. (Optional) Testing the connection to destination using Entity's list operation
      1. You can test the connection to your DOX destination by creating adding a REST API data entity and use it to call one of the GET API available in your service, assuming you have the right access. Otherwise, you can skip this steps or choose different API to test with.
      2. Press on the "Add REST API Data Entity" button. You should get a dialog pop up.
      3. In the dialog, enter the following information:
        • Name: Documents
        • Autogenerate configuration: enabled
        • Entity path within the API: /?cmisselector=children
        • Enable Capabilities: enable "list" and uncheck the rest of them
        • Press "Add" button
      4. You might see a warning about some troubles with the resource schema. You can safely ignore that as we are only using this as verification.
      5. You should now see the Documents entity and its Fields and more.
        If you are seeing other error here and it couldn't generate any Fields for you, then it's likely there's something wrong with your path, or destination configuration. Make sure to check your destination's URL and authentication configuration.
      6. You can use "Browse Real Data" button to check if you can see expected data entries and no errors.
      7. If you are not seeing any errors, it means the connection to your destination is good for usage.
    6. You can now Exit this page.

Using HTTP Destination Request to Upload File

  1. Go to “User Interface” tab.
  2. Press on the button (or any other UI control) that you wish to use to trigger the file upload and then press on the "Add logic to <your control>" at the bottom of the screen. This should show the Logic Canvas.
  3. Press the “Marketplace” button under the "Core" tab on the left panel of the Logic Canvas.
    1. Search for “Pick files” flow function and install it and then re-open the Marketplace again.
    2. Search for “HTTP Destination Request” flow function and install it.
    3. After both are installed you should see them in the "INSTALLED" tab of the Logic Canvas.
  4. You are now ready to use “HTTP destination request” to upload file:
    1. Go to the "INSTALLED" tab of the Logic Canvas.
    2. Drag “Pick files” to the logic flow canvas.
    3. Link the button's “Event: Component tap” node to the “Pick files” node.
    4. Drag “HTTP destination request” to the logic flow canvas.
    5. Link “Pick files” node’s output port 1 to the “HTTP destination request” node, because we want to pass the files selected by user to the “HTTP destination request” node.
    6. Click on the “HTTP destination request” node and on its property panel.
      1. In the Destination dropdown select your installed destination.
      2. Set the “HTTP method” as “POST”.
      3. Set the “Request body type” as “multipart/form-data”.
      4. Set the “Request body” and add entries for each part needed for the target API. In this case, we will be using the DMS Create Document API which requires 10 properties:
        KeyValue
        cmisactioncreateDocument
        propertyId[0]cmis:name
        propertyValue[0]Bind this to formula using the following: outputs[“Pick “files].files[0].name
        propertyId[1]cmis:objectTypeId
        propertyValue[1]cmis:document
        filenamebind this to formula using the following: outputs[“Pick “files].files[0].name
        _charset_UTF-8
        includeAllowableActionstrue
        succinctfalse
        media bind this to formula using the following: outputs[“Pick “files].files[0]
        Note: “Pick files” flow function allows user to pick one or more files. In this scenario, we will only take the first item of the selected files by accessing index 0, hence the files[0]
        For example:
      5. Your "HTTP destination request" is now ready.
      6. ℹ️ Optional Inputs
        In the Optional Inputs section of the"HTTP destination request", you can also specify additional "Path" that’s relative to your destination, if needed. The path specified here will be appended to your destination in the final request. In this example, we don’t need to specify path as our destination is already pointing to the target API path. However, for DMS create document API you may append inner folder path, if you wish, e.g. /FolderOne
        You can also specify additional HTTP "Headers" here.
    7. Drag and drop 1 "Toast" flow function to the Logic Canvas and link them to each of the output port 1 of the “HTTP destination request” flow function.
      For Toast linked to port 1 you can bind its message to formula with the following:
      "Result: " + outputs["HTTP destination request"].successStatusText
    8. Drag and drop 2 "Alert" flow function to the Logic Canvas and link them to each of the output ports of the “HTTP destination request” flow function.
      1. For the Alert linked to port 2, you can bind its message to formula with the following:
        "Error from backend: " + outputs["HTTP destination request"].failureStatusText
      2. For the Alert linked to port 3, you can bind its message to formula with the following:
        "Error: " + outputs["HTTP destination request"].error
    9. The logic canvas should looks like this:
    10. Save your project and you are ready to preview your app and test the file upload.

Preview and Trigger File Upload

  • Press on the "Preview" button > Open web preview and choose your app.
  • When the app is loaded, press the file upload button:
  • Pick a file from your file system and press Open:
  • After succesful upload you should get "Result: Created" toast:
  • You should see the file now uploaded to your target repository.
  • If possible, check your repository for the uploaded file.

Summary

As summary, in this blog, you have learned how to set up your Build Apps project to make use of the "HTTP destination request" and "Pick files" to upload file using multipart/form-data.

If you are interested with uploading file to other services such as SAP Document Information Extraction Service using "Take photo"and "HTTP destination request" in SAP Build Apps, you can visit this blog: SAP Build Apps - upload files to SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX) Service repository with camera.

entry.title: Global User ID in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
The Global User ID (userUUID) plays an important role in integration scenarios, acting as a consistent user identifier across system landscapes. This is especially important for scenarios like API-based notification services, where a common identifier ensures seamless data flow and user correlation.

There are two approaches for implementing the Global User ID, but we recommend the first of the two:

Approach 1:

Use SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication and Identity Provisioning to generate and distribute Global User ID. In this case, the attribute is automatically generated by Identity Authentication at user creation. Its value is populated in the Global User ID field for every newly created, imported or provisioned user. After that, Identity Provisioning distributes it to various SAP applications.

Approach 2:

Use your own value for Global User ID and distribute it to SAP applications with SAP Cloud Identity Services. In this case the Identity Authentication is used as a user store, it receives the value in the Global User ID field for every newly created, imported or provisioned user. After that, Identity Provisioning distributes it to various SAP applications. Because the Global User ID field cannot be empty, if you do not provide a value for Global User ID, Identity Authentication generates one for your users. When distributing your own value without SAP Cloud Identity Services, you use your existing identity management processes to provision it to SAP applications (see  the dotted line in the diagram below).

 

Prerequisites and usage

  • Users must have Identity Authentication and Identity Provisioning configured in their landscape.
  • The Global User ID identifier is immutable and unique across technology layers such as user interfaces, APIs, and security tokens, as well as across products and lines of business.
  • It is recommended to use the Global User ID of SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication as the federation identifier.
  • Identity Authentication hosts the Global User ID while Identity Provisioning provisions the Global User ID to other systems in the user’s landscape.

 

How to leverage Global ID in SAP Build Work Zone

Provisioning the Global User ID

First, the Global ID is provisioned to SAP Build Work Zone via SCIM API, and consequently transferred to SAP Build Work Zone, as to any other SAP solution, via Identity Provisioning. This applies to both:
