  • Role-mapping for content providers within SAP Build Work Zone, all editions as an alternative to role collection-based mapping. See here for more details.
  • Digital Workplace Service (DWS) persistence layer specific to SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. This process is part of the Work Zone onboarding. More details about this can be found here.

The Global User ID is transferred (in both scenarios) via the target system transformation / mapping in Identity Provisioning. Using the provided (default) transformation from the SAP Build Work Zone help guide will ensure the attribute is provisioned as required. You will need to verify that the Identity Provisioning source system (for example Cloud Identity) has the Global User ID available.

For example, SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition target system:

 

Trust Setup and Subject Name Identifier

It is recommended to use the Global User ID as the subject name identifier for the trust setup - for both the BTP Subaccount level and direct trust with SAP Build Work Zone. If you want to learn more about the direct OpenID Connect-based trust setup, please see the following blog.

Depending on the configuration of the ‘JAM’ destination, specifically the userIdSource property, it will also require the Global User ID in additional fields – specifically the SCIM.userName. To learn more about the authentication flow and corresponding field usage, please see here.

 

Verify Global User ID information in SAP Build Work Zone (advanced)

To verify the Global ID is properly created in the SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition / SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone, you can check the users using and directly modifying when needed, the SCIM API. This is the same API also utilized by Identity Provisioning (Work Zone target system). When using the API, you can find the Global User ID stored in a dedicated field in the SCIM user record:

For more details related to the SCIM API of advanced edition, how to connect to it, and more, please see the dedicated section in the help guide.

Finally, you can run a report in SAP Build Work Zone administration console by selecting the Company User Detail report (for both internal and external users). This will provide you with the list of all users provisioned to the system and the user values for SCIM.userName, SCIM.id (and others) which can be used as filters / parameters in the SCIM API. Note that in the report the User ID refers to the SCIM.userName and the Uuid refers to SCIM.id.

 

Use Cases

Global User ID plays an important role in SAP Build Work Zone, especially in integration scenarios. For instance, in SAP Task Center, the Global User ID functions as a correlation attribute, linking user identities across different systems. This ensures that tasks from multiple systems can be reliably associated with the same user, enabling a unified task management experience. The integration with the Central Notification Service, which allows Work Zone users to access notifications from various systems, also requires the Global User ID mapping.

Details on this integration setup can be found here (for Task Center) & here (for Notifications).

Another worth mentioning use case is Guided Experiences, where the Global User ID is needed to communicate between SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA), and SAP Build Work Zone. This is instrumental for the UI Wizard in SAP Build Work Zone to retrieve and utilise processes created in SBPA, streamlining workflows and ensuring smooth integration across systems.

Lastly, when creating UI integration cards, the Global User ID is also provided / exposed as user context and can be referenced by the developer of the UI Cards. More details on this built-in card context is available in the SAP-samples GitHub repository:

 

For more info please check:

Help Guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-advanced-edition/sap-build-work-zone-advanced-edition/run-configurator

Learning Journey & Certification: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implement-and-administer-sap-build-work-zone/explaining-the-authentication-flow-of-sap-build-work-zone

entry.title: SAP Build产品功能更新目录
entry.content:
➡️SAP Build 中的新增功能 – 2024 年第 4 季度版本

亮点功能：

  • SAP Build流程自动化在流程中通过 Actions 使用 RFC / BAPI

  • SAP Build Code 移动开发工具包（MDK）中的生成式 AI 功能增强
  • Joule 与 Work Zone 标准版的集成
  • 引导式流程向导

 

➡️SAP Build 中的新增功能 – 2024 年第 3 季度版本

亮点功能：

  • 在SAP S/4HANA Cloud中创建扩展流程
  • 跨 SAP Build Work Zone 和 Process Automation 的集成引导式体验

 

➡️SAP Build 的新增功能 – 2024 年第 2 季度版本 

亮点功能：

  • SAP Build Apps 与 SAP Build Process Automation 之间的集成功能
  • SAP Task Center连接第三方任务提供商
  • 从 SAP Intelligent RPA 轻松迁移到 SAP Build Process Automation

 

➡️SAP Build Process Automation 2024 年 1 月新功能详解

亮点功能：

  • 在垂直布局流程编辑器中使用布局的自动化、决策和流程可见性
  • 增强录制器中的不同类型的设置活动选项

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q4 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Welcome to the Q4 2024 edition of our "What's New "blog series. SAP Build is a business application development and automation solution with a comprehensive suite of low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. This quarter, we're excited to highlight many new releases, including AI-driven features that transform your ability to automate and innovate. Let's dive in!

Below, you will find product-specific highlights for:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Code
  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Process Automation

Create and Manage Process Variants

SAP Build Process Automation now allows you to create and manage process variants. With pre-defined process steps and guardrails, process experts and professional developers can model different variants for different user groups using a configuration-only experience. By defining variant conditions and actions, workflows can be adapted to your specific business needs. With this feature, you can manage tailored workflows for various scenarios without impacting the overall process template. Learn how to create and manage process variants in the SAP Help Portal.

Consume RFC / BAPI in a Process via Actions

To help you extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-premise solution, you can now create Actions based on RFCs / BAPIs. Utilizing the BAPI node library, you can directly incorporate RFC actions, streamlining the integration process. Easily search and browse RFC actions to locate the necessary functions for your workflows, maintain system integrity, and reduce complexity.

 

SAP Build Code

Generative AI Capabilities in the Mobile Development Kit

Enhanced generative AI capabilities for the Mobile Development Kit (MDK) can now help you simplify your app creation process. Developers can:

  • Generate Application Logic: Automatically produce metadata-driven logic, reducing manual coding efforts.
  • Create Forms and Workflows: Quickly develop forms and workflows with AI assistance, accelerating development.
  • Build Robust Mobile Solutions: Leverage AI to minimize manual effort, allowing focus on delivering impactful solutions.

Learn more in this blog by @BirgitS. 

SAPUI5 Development with TypeScript and UI5 Linter

AI-Driven Migration to TypeScript with Joule: Joule, the AI assistant integrated into SAP Build Code, now enables a seamless migration of SAPUI5 applications from JavaScript to TypeScript. TypeScript offers enhanced development efficiency, providing a structured and scalable approach to building SAPUI5 apps. Learn more about this powerful capability in this blog by @martinhaeuser.

Release of UI5 Linter v1.0: SAP Build Code introduces UI5 Linter v1.0, a tool designed to ensure code quality and alignment with best practices. This release empowers developers to write cleaner, more maintainable SAPUI5 code, enhancing the overall development lifecycle.

A New Experience for Extending SAP Fiori Apps

Adaptation Editor

Adaptation Editor for SAPUI5 Projects: SAP Build Code now features the Adaptation Editor, a new tool for extending SAPUI5 applications. Replacing the SAPUI5 Visual Editor, the Adaptation Editor is available in SAP Business Application Studio, offering an intuitive and consistent experience across environments. Developers can leverage this updated tool for streamlined SAPUI5 extensions with improved performance. For details, see the announcement blog by @OliverGraeff. 

Adaptation vs. Extension Projects: SAPUI5 flexibility supports both adaptation and extension projects, enabling tailored app modifications for developers, key users, and end users. Adaptation projects, now the strategic approach for extending SAPUI5 apps, provide advanced tools for code-based customization. To learn about the differences and benefits of these project types, explore this comparison blog.

SAP Build Apps

Additional HTTP Request Headers for BTP Destinations

Gain greater control and flexibility when integrating external services with SAP Build Apps. You can now configure additional HTTP request headers for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) destinations, enhancing integration security and customization. The new flow function allows you to upload binary files using multipart/form-data as request body type. To learn more, see HTTP Destination Request flow function reference documentation.

A New Top Navigation Bar

SAP Build Apps now has an updated toolbar and navigation menu for a more intuitive experience. Frequently used features like Variables are now accessible directly from the toolbar. Tabs like "UI Canvas" and "Data" have been renamed to "User Interface" and "Integrations." At the same time, less-used options such as Theme and Navigation are grouped under "App Settings" to declutter the interface. Actions like Preview and Publish are prominently placed in the upper-right corner, simplifying release versioning and deployment. For more details, read the feature review blog post by @Dan_Wroblewski.

SAP Build Work Zone

Integration with Joule for Work Zone Standard Edition

Joule is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition, enhancing the workspace with AI-powered capabilities. This integration delivers intelligent workspace recommendations to boost efficiency, proactive insights tailored to tasks and priorities, and streamlined workflows to improve collaboration and focus. For more examples of how Joule can transform your workspaces, check out @Yumnahabbasi's blog post.

Guided Process Wizard

The Guided Process Wizard combines SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation to simplify creating structured, step-by-step workflows. With this integration, you can design guided processes in Process Automation and deploy them as wizards in Work Zone. Build customizable workflows with dynamic UI elements like cards and widgets to streamline complex onboarding and invoice processing tasks. Guided experiences are included in the SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. Read more in the FAQ blogpost by @Yumnahabbasi.

Learn More

For a complete list of new features, check out the following resources:

  • What's New Viewer
  • SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Road Map Explorer

To stay updated with the latest announcements and innovations, follow the SAP Builders Community and converse with fellow SAP Builders.

We look forward to seeing how you leverage these new capabilities to achieve your goals!

entry.title: Understand DMS API – Free Postman Collection!!
entry.content:
In my videos introducing the Document Management Service, I received some comments asking for the Postman Collection I used to show how the API works.

So I decided to release Postman collection, and write a short blog post to explain how the APIs work.

You can see all my DMS videos in the DMS playlist - more to come soon: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hLrCoHPwqT4&list=PL6RpkC85SLQCsKSvIOqMzFKW30B4TKXh-

 

How the APIs Work

There's 2 API Sets

The APIs are described on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, but lo and behold, there are 2 separate sets of APIs:

  • CMIS Compliant – API Reference: Most of the APIs for working with content are compliant with CMIS, an industry standard for APIs that call content management systems. 
     
  • SAP REST API – API Reference: These have more to do with creating a repo and administrative tasks.

Objects

The contents of the repo are objects, which contain CMIS-defined properties. Some of the more notable properties:

  • cmis:objectId: Unique ID for that object
  • cmis:objectTypeId: Indicates the type of object, generally document (cmis:document) or folder (cmis:folder)
  • cmis:name: Name for that object
  • cmis:parentIds: Object ID of the folders that contain this object
  • ... you can also get the owner, creation date, modified date, version, mime type of document type and more. DMS has complex capabilities, like version or encryption, and those are beyond the scope of this blog post.

 

"properties": {
	"cmis:objectId": {
		"id": "cmis:objectId",
		"localName": "cmis:objectId",
		"displayName": "cmis:objectId",
		"queryName": "cmis:objectId",
		"type": "id",
		"cardinality": "single",
		"value": "DzX04u_1PD5uNT2IfcmNzztROjky-92pHkAK1-SoE5U"
	},
	"cmis:name": {
		"id": "cmis:name",
		"localName": "cmis:name",
		"displayName": "cmis:name",
		"queryName": "cmis:name",
		"type": "string",
		"cardinality": "single",
		"value": "invoice_id-1731489392337-28"
	},
	"cmis:objectTypeId": {
		"id": "cmis:objectTypeId",
		"localName": "cmis:objectTypeId",
		"displayName": "cmis:objectTypeId",
		"queryName": "cmis:objectTypeId",
		"type": "id",
		"cardinality": "single",
		"value": "cmis:folder"
	}
}

 

Note that the property names start with cmis. When working with the properties, you need to use this namespace. This affects how you write some of the URLs (such as the query call) as well as selecting properties in the formula editor of SAP Build Apps (see below).

SAP Build Apps Formula Editor

Since the property names have a colon in them (:), you will need to use an alternate form for specifying those fields in the formula editor of SAP Build Apps.

For example, if you use the standard format, the formula editor will give an error:

Instead, you need to put such properties inside parentheses:

Queries

All but one of the calls are standard REST calls, and similar to many other REST APIs. But there is a query call, that lets you write SQL-esque statements to retrieve objects. 

For example, if I want to retrieve all of the documents for a folder with a specific ID, and return only the name property, I could use the following URL: 

 

https://{{endpoint}}/browser/{{repo}}?cmisSelector=query&
q=SELECT cmis:name FROM cmis:folder WHERE
cmis:objectId='DzX04u_1PD5uNT2IfcmNzztROjky-92pHkAK1-SoE5U'

 

To get the full OASIS documentation on writing queries, see their documentation.

 

Postman Collection

You can download the collection here: https://github.com/sap-tutorials/sap-build-apps/blob/main/projects/dms/DMS_CMIS.postman_collection.json

NOTE:

  • Some of the requests in the collection contain example objectIDs or file names, which of course will not work on your tenants. So you will have to change those.

Create Environment

In order to use the requests, you will need to set up an environment that supplies some of the authentication information. What I did is set up an environment for every different tenant I had that included DMS.

In the environment, you'll need to fill in the following properties:

 

clientid
clientsecret
endpoint
authenticationurl
repo

 

The first 4 you will get from the service key of your DMS Integration Option service.

The service key will look something like this:

On the second, authentication url property at the bottom, you'll have to append oauh/token to the value.

The repo property you get when you run the API for all your repos:

Get Token

The authentication for all the requests is set up to be done in the parent node, the top of the collection, DMS CMIS.

All you have to do is scroll down and click Get New Access Token.

If all goes well, you should get a success message.

Then click Use Token.

Occasionally, I find that the authentication doesn't work and I get an unauthorized message because there is a cookie that is not being overwritten. So for that request, I clear the cookie. But this doesn't happen very often.

Click Cookies (you may have to click twice), and then delete the cookie.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, January 9th, 2025
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/01/09/sap-developer-news-january-9th-2025/

DESCRIPTION

Welcome to 2025

Mastering SAP S/4HANA Extension with SAP Build- Discovery center mission

  • New Discovery Center mission for SAP Build: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4420/4706/
  • BTP Discovery Center: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/

What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud 2024/Q4

  • What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – December 2024: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-hana-cloud-december-2024/ba-p/13967275
  • New Machine Learning and NLP features in SAP HANA Cloud 2024 Q4: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/new-machine-learning-and-nlp-features-in-sap-hana-cloud-2024-q4/ba-p/13953934
  • Calculation View Features of 2024 QRC4: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/calculation-view-features-of-2024-qrc4/ba-p/13965370

SAP Open Source Report 2024

  • Blog post “A year of Collaboration and Innovation: SAP Open Source Report 2024”: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/a-year-of-collaboration-and-innovation-sap-open-source-report-2024/ba-p/13978967
  • SAP Open Source Report 2024: https://d.dam.sap.com/a/wxgtG2E/SAP%20Open%20Source%202024%20Report.pdf

Video: UI5 Web Components 2.0

  • https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1ERrvS5-MpM

CAP Node.js Plugins live stream mini-series

  • The series post with all the links to the live streams, recordings and blog posts: https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2024/12/30/cap-node.js-plugins/

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 Welcome to 2025

1:35 Discovery center mission “Mastering SAP S/4HANA Extension with SAP Build”

2:39 What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud 2024/Q4

4:46 SAP Open Source Report 2024

5:35 Video: UI5 Web Components 2.0

5:59 CAP Node.js Plugins live stream mini-series

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for January 9th, 2025.

[Thomas] Welcome everybody to the developer news in 2025. We've got more of the same great little newsbytes coming your way every week. We want to keep you up to date on what's going on in the SAP world for all of our developer ecosystem. But we also have some great new things planned for this year. We're going to continue with the CodeJams, of course. We want to bring up. Bring more road shows to areas that we've not been able to visit in the past couple years. We also will continue the TechEd on Tour, bringing the TechEd in-person experience to more locations near you. Be on the lookout for some of those locations announced probably about mid-year because most of that will happen in the fourth quarter of this year. And we've got some fun new things planned for the developer challenges this year as well. Already by mid-January, so in another week or so, you'll be able to try out the new SAP Community Escape Room Challenge. Can't wait for you to see what fun that's going to be. We've got some other great new things planned as well. We've got some new CodeJams on advanced topics that we're working on, and we'll continue to bring you all the same great experiences on the SAP developers YouTube channel that you've come to expect. So we look forward toa great 2025 with you.

[Rekha] Hello, everybody. Are you using SAP BTP or have plans to use SAP BTP in the future, then SAP BTP Discovery Center website is a must-know resource. The Discovery Center has missions with use cases, step-by-step guides, reference architecture, SAP BTPs related details, and information related to newly added SAP Business AI features. A new mission called mastering SAP S/4HANA extensions with SAP Build has been made available in the Discovery Center. Here you find the use case, managing incident reporting and resolution, the SAP BTP services and components that are required to realize this use case. You also get access to SAP expert support and SAP community support while learning about this mission. Do check this out on the Discovery Center. Links are provided in the description box. Keep learning. Thank you.

[Witalij] During the holiday season, I celebrated my birthday on December 23rd. And the SAP HANA product team marked the occasion with a special surprise for me and every one of you, the Q4, 24 release of a SAP HANA Cloud. This release brings a host of features designed to benefit data scientists, developers, and AI engineers alike. The Q42024 release introduces native multi-tenancy at the database level. This allows customer data to be managed in database tenants that share the same database instance while remaining logically separated. Multi-tenancy capability ensures enterprise-grade qualities at the tenant level, including customer-specific encryption, recovery, and resource isolation. With every quarterly release, the SAP HANA Cloud predictive analytics library, or PAL, has been enhanced with new embedded machine learning AI and NLP functions. Key highlights of this release include the introduction of text analysis and text authorization, as well as expanded support for vector data as input for additional machine learning functions such as classification and regression. Among the new calculation view features, the graphical expression editor null offers vector functions as well, such as causal similarity to calculate the similarity between the vectors, as well as the function to create a vector embedding from the text, using the vector embedding function obviously. The new SPL plan advisor enables the identification of performance issues in deviating SQL statements. It detects and optimizes SQL performance by comparing execution engine plans. By automatically testing and comparing execution performance across different plans, the advisor recommends and applies optimized execution plans for improved SQL performance. As always, be sure to check the blog posts from SAP Product Managers for all the details. Links are provided in the description. Wishing you a peaceful 2025. Bye. Bye.

[DJ] 2024 was a great year for SAP and the Open Source Program Office and their activities. They've just published the Open Source Program Office and their activities. the SAP Open Source Report 24, which has some great insights as well as listing many of their accomplishments. And having read that report myself, I come away with the key concepts being all about people, community, commitment and collaboration. Basically, it's all about working together in the ecosphere. Anyway, there's a blog post from Ulrika about. the report and also the report itself linked both in the description. Happy reading.

[Nico] Hi everyone and welcome to the SAP developer news. You would like to point your attention to a cool video from the UI5 core team about the UI5 Web Components 2.x release, including the new popover API, new AI components, and the enhanced toolkit. So go check it out and hope to see you soon.

[DJ] We just wanted to let you know that right now we're running a new three-part live stream series on the Hands on SAP Dev show on our YouTube channel, all about CAP Node.js plugins. We had part one last week, which was all about understanding how the plug-in mechanism works. Part two is this week where we learn to all about understanding how the plug-in mechanism works. introspect our service in the CDS repel and the final part three is next week where we actually use the knowledge we've gained in the first two parts and write our plugin. Anyway, there's a link to a series post in the description to this video. In that series post there are links to the live streams, the replays and also to the accompanying blog posts. See you online. Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Build 中的新增功能 – 2024 年第 4 季度版本
entry.content:
更快、更智能、更高效地构建

欢迎阅读我们2024年第四季度的“产品新功能”博客系列。本文为翻译文，原文链接What’s New in SAP Build – Q4 2024 Edition，原文作者JesseSukhbayar。

SAP Build 是一款业务应用程序开发和自动化解决方案，提供了一套全面的低代码、专业代码和生成式 AI 工具。本季度，我们很高兴为大家介绍许多新版本，其中包括 AI 驱动的功能，这些功能将彻底改变您的自动化和创新能力。让我们一起来看看吧！

以下内容将为您介绍以下产品的具体亮点：

  • SAP Build Process Automation

  • SAP Build Code

  • SAP Build Apps

  • SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build 流程自动化

创建和管理流程变体

SAP Build 流程自动化现在支持创建和管理流程变体。通过预定义的流程步骤和防护机制，流程专家和专业开发人员可以使用纯配置的方式为不同的用户群体建模不同的流程变体。通过定义变体条件和操作，工作流可以根据您的具体业务需求进行调整。借助此功能，您可以为各种场景管理定制化的工作流，而不会影响整体流程模板。

您可以在 SAP 帮助门户 中了解如何创建和管理流程变体。

在流程中通过 Actions 使用 RFC / BAPI

为了帮助您扩展 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 私有版和本地部署解决方案，您现在可以基于 RFC / BAPI 创建 Actions。通过利用 BAPI 节点库，您可以直接集成 RFC Actions，从而简化集成流程。您可以轻松搜索和浏览 RFC Actions，以找到工作流所需的必要功能，同时维护系统完整性并降低复杂性。

这一功能使您能够更高效地将 SAP S/4HANA 的功能集成到自动化流程中，从而提升业务操作的灵活性和效率。

SAP Build Code

移动开发工具包（MDK）中的生成式 AI 功能增强

移动开发工具包（MDK）的生成式 AI 功能现已增强，可以帮助您简化应用程序创建过程。开发人员可以：

  1. 生成应用程序逻辑：自动生成基于元数据的逻辑，减少手动编码工作量。

  2. 创建表单和工作流：在 AI 的协助下快速开发表单和工作流，加速开发进程。

  3. 构建强大的移动解决方案：利用 AI 减少手动操作，专注于交付具有影响力的解决方案。

通过这些功能，开发人员可以更高效地构建移动应用，同时将更多精力投入到业务价值的实现中。

了解更多详情，请阅读 @BirgitS 的博客 。

SAPUI5 开发：TypeScript 和 UI5 Linter

使用 Joule 进行 AI 驱动的 TypeScript 迁移

Joule 是集成到 SAP Build Code 中的 AI 助手，现在支持将 SAPUI5 应用程序从 JavaScript 无缝迁移到 TypeScript。TypeScript 提供了更高的开发效率，为构建 SAPUI5 应用程序提供了一种结构化且可扩展的方法。了解更多关于这一强大功能的信息，请阅读 @martinhaeuser 的博客 。

UI5 Linter v1.0 发布

SAP Build Code 推出了 UI5 Linter v1.0，这是一款旨在确保代码质量并与最佳实践保持一致的工具。此版本使开发人员能够编写更清晰、更易维护的 SAPUI5 代码，从而提升整体开发生命周期的效率。

通过这些新功能，开发人员可以更高效地构建高质量的 SAPUI5 应用程序，同时减少错误并提高代码的可维护性。

扩展 SAP Fiori 应用程序的全新体验

适用于 SAPUI5 项目的 Adaptation Editor

SAP Build Code 现在推出了 Adaptation Editor，这是一款用于扩展 SAPUI5 应用程序的新工具。Adaptation Editor 取代了 SAPUI5 Visual Editor，并在 SAP Business Application Studio 中提供，为跨环境开发提供了直观且一致的体验。开发人员可以利用这一更新后的工具，以更高的性能简化 SAPUI5 扩展。详情请参阅 @OliverGraeff 的博客 。

适应项目与扩展项目

SAPUI5 的灵活性支持适应项目（Adaptation Projects）和扩展项目（Extension Projects），使开发人员、关键用户和最终用户能够对应用程序进行定制化修改。适应项目现已成为扩展 SAPUI5 应用程序的战略性方法，提供了基于代码定制的高级工具。要了解这些项目类型的区别和优势，请参阅 这篇博客 。

通过这些新工具和方法，开发人员可以更灵活、高效地扩展和定制 SAPUI5 应用程序，满足多样化的业务需求。

SAP Build Apps

为 BTP 目标服务添加额外的 HTTP 请求

在将外部服务与 SAP Build Apps 集成时，您现在可以配置额外的 HTTP 请求头，从而获得更高的控制性和灵活性。这一功能增强了集成安全性和定制化能力。此外，新的流函数（flow function）支持使用 multipart/form-data 作为请求体类型上传二进制文件。

要了解更多详细信息，请参阅 HTTP Destination Request 流函数参考文档。

通过这些更新，开发人员可以更安全、更灵活地集成外部服务，并支持更复杂的文件上传场景。

全新的顶部导航栏

SAP Build Apps 现在提供了更新的工具栏和导航菜单，为用户带来更直观的体验。常用的功能（如“变量”）现在可以直接从工具栏访问。同时，部分标签名称进行了更新，例如：

  • “UI Canvas” 更名为 “用户界面”

  • “Data” 更名为 “集成”

此外，使用频率较低的选项（如“主题”和“导航”）被归类到 “应用设置” 中，以减少界面杂乱感。常用操作（如“预览”和“发布”）则被显著地放置在右上角，简化了版本管理和部署流程。

要了解更多详细信息，请阅读 @Dan_Wroblewski 的功能评测博客 。

通过这些改进，SAP Build Apps 的用户界面更加简洁高效，帮助开发人员更专注于应用程序的设计和开发。

SAP Build Work Zone

Joule 与 Work Zone 标准版的集成

Joule 现已集成到 SAP Build Work Zone 标准版 中，为工作区提供了 AI 驱动的强大功能。这一集成带来了以下优势：

  • 智能工作区推荐：提升工作效率。

  • 主动洞察：根据任务和优先级提供定制化建议。

  • 简化的工作流：改善协作并帮助用户更专注于关键任务。

通过 Joule 的 AI 能力，用户可以更高效地管理工作区，并获得个性化的支持。

要了解更多关于 Joule 如何改变您的工作区的示例，请阅读 @Yumnahabbasi 的博客 。

引导式流程向导

引导式流程向导（Guided Process Wizard）结合了 SAP Build Work Zone 和 SAP Build 流程自动化，简化了创建结构化、分步工作流的过程。通过这一集成，您可以在流程自动化中设计引导式流程，并将其作为向导部署到 Work Zone 中。

您可以利用动态 UI 元素（如卡片和小部件）构建可定制的工作流，从而简化复杂的入职和发票处理等任务。引导式体验功能包含在 SAP Build Work Zone 高级版 和 SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone 中。

要了解更多详细信息，请阅读 @Yumnahabbasi 的 FAQ 博客。

 

了解更多

如需查看完整的新功能列表，请访问以下资源：

  • What's New Viewer（新功能查看器）

  • SAP 帮助门户

  • SAP Road Map Explorer（ SAP 路线图探索器）

为了及时了解最新公告和创新动态，请关注 SAP Builders Community，并与社区中的其他 SAP Builders 交流互动。

entry.title: Navigating SAP Customizations: A Step-by-Step approach to a Clean(er) Core with S/4HANA
entry.content:
When I was a kid, I read a note at my doctor’s clinic:

“On one side, my livelihood depends on people’s suffering, but on the other, I am grateful that I’ve been given the opportunity to relieve their pain and help them feel better.”

That sentiment has always stayed with me—especially now that I am a SAP consultant. While I benefit professionally from helping companies that struggle with complex SAP solutions, I am driven by a genuine desire to ease those struggles, maximize the return on their SAP investments, and deliver real business value. In other words, I want to help “heal” their pain points.

In this post, I am sharing a framework for customizing SAP without overcomplicating the system—a way to minimize the “suffering” that stems from excessive modifications while still delivering the specific functionalities your organization needs.

1. Apply a Zero-Modification Policy

 

  • What It Means: Refrain from modifying standard SAP code. SAP S/4HANA and other SAP solutions come with well-established best practices, so start by leveraging standard features to the fullest extent.
  • Key Benefit: Avoiding direct changes to standard code reduces the risk of upgrade and patch conflicts. With fewer dependencies, you can adopt new SAP functionalities more quickly and smoothly.

 

2. Evaluate If Key-User Extensibility Fits

 

  • How It Works: SAP offers key-user tools (called “In-App Extensibility”) that let power users modify fields, adapt forms, and tweak the UI—all without deep coding.
  • Why It Matters: This approach is faster and less invasive. Because it lives within the SAP-recommended extensibility framework, updates and patches remain simpler.

 

Key Metrics to Track

 

  • Time-to-Implement: Measure how quickly you can deploy changes using key-user tools versus classical ABAP developments.
  • User Adoption Rate: Assess whether key users find these tools intuitive and how much IT intervention is needed.

 

3. Identify Where Customizations Are Truly Needed

 

  • What This Step Means: If key-user tools don’t fulfill your requirements, consider a more advanced approach—but verify that deeper customization is genuinely necessary.
  • Guiding Questions:

 

Can an existing process be adjusted or reconfigured to avoid new custom code?

Does a standard SAP or third-party solution address your needs?

Common Pitfall

“Customization Creep”: Over-engineering even minor requirements can pile on technical debt. Always check if SAP’s out-of-the-box capabilities—or minor configuration adjustments—can solve the problem first.

4. Analyze If the Customization Is Necessary

 

  • Why This Matters: Identifying a gap doesn’t always mean you should fill it with custom code. This step evaluates whether the benefits truly outweigh the costs. Even a legitimate need can become a burden if the maintenance, testing, and upgrade overhead outweigh the functionality gained.
  • Evaluation Factors:

 

Business Value: Will new features measurably enhance productivity, compliance, or revenue?

Long-Term Impact: How likely is it that future SAP updates could break this customization? Is the maintenance effort justifiable over time?

Conduct a Cost-Benefit Analysis. Factor in both development and maintenance costs, then compare them to the potential ROI—be it time savings, reduced errors, or improved profitability. This ensures you only invest in customizations that deliver clear, long-term value.

5. Embrace Side-by-Side Extensibility

 

  • What It Is: Instead of embedding code directly into the SAP core, build extensions on external platforms such as SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).
  • Advantages:

 

Isolation: Keeps the core ERP stable by separating custom logic.

Scalability: Cloud-based apps can adapt as your business grows or evolves.

Reusability: APIs and microservices can be repurposed across multiple SAP and non-SAP systems.

6. Resort to Classical ABAP Customization When Necessary

 

  • When to Use It: For highly specialized or performance-critical needs that aren’t met by in-app or side-by-side solutions.
  • Best Practices:

 

Deviation Tracker: Keep a record of what changed, why, and who approved it.

Use Enhancement Points: SAP-provided “BAdIs” (Business Add-Ins) or enhancement frameworks reduce the risk of breaking standard code.

Governance & Testing: Maintain strict code reviews and regular regression tests to avoid introducing systemic issues.

Periodically review your ABAP custom objects. Some might be replaced by new standard features in the latest SAP release, helping you reduce technical debt.

Clean Core Challenges

1. Cultural Mindset

 

  • Challenge: A longstanding belief that customization is the only way to meet exact requirements.
  • Solution: Promote an ROI-based decision-making process, showing that fewer modifications often speed up upgrades and reduce costs.

 

2. Legacy Custom Code

 

  • Challenge: Older systems may already have extensive, mission-critical customizations.
  • Solution: Identify and gradually retire or refactor outdated code. New SAP features may now serve the same purpose more elegantly.

 

3. Complex Business Requirements

 

  • Challenge: Some processes—especially in regulated industries—exceed what standard tools can offer.
  • Solution: Use side-by-side extensibility or developer extensibility judiciously, ensuring robust design practices and strong governance.

 

4. Limitations of Clean Core APIs and BAdIs

 

  • Challenge: Modern APIs and BAdIs may lack the depth and flexibility of classic equivalents, limiting their ability to address complex requirements.
  • Solution: Analyze gaps early in the design phase and leverage side-by-side extensions or custom APIs to address unmet needs. Monitor SAP's roadmap for upcoming enhancements.

 

5. Governance & Policy Adherence

 

  • Challenge: Under tight deadlines, teams might sidestep zero-modification policies for quick fixes.
  • Solution: Create a governance board and standardized templates for cost-benefit analyses to maintain consistency.

 

6. Perceived Limitations of Standard

 

  • Challenge: Users might assume SAP’s standard capabilities are too limited.
  • Solution: Provide demos or proofs of concept to show how versatile SAP actually is. Accelerate your integrations, extensions, and innovations with SAP Business Accelerator Hub

 

7. Balancing Innovation and Stability

 

  • Challenge: Teams want rapid innovation without risking core stability.
  • Solution: Keep your core as untouched as possible and innovate externally—for instance, using agile development on SAP BTP.

 

Measuring Success

 

  1. Customization Density The ratio of custom objects to standard objects—lower is typically better for smooth upgrades, since fewer custom dependencies mean fewer points of conflict.
  2. Upgrade Cycle Efficiency The time needed to adapt custom code during new releases or patches.
  3. Incidents Tracked How many production issues are tied to custom code vs. standard functionality.

 

Legacy Modernization Strategy

For organizations dealing with large swaths of legacy code, map each customization’s usage and importance. Often, newer SAP enhancements or frameworks replicate or surpass older custom logic. Prioritize retiring or refactoring the highest-cost, least-used customizations first for immediate gains in stability and maintenance efficiency.

Much like the doctor’s note that inspired me as a child—balancing the inevitability of pain with the privilege of providing relief—SAP customization should aim to resolve genuine “pain points” without creating new ones. By embracing a zero-modification mindset, leveraging key-user and side-by-side extensibility, and judiciously using classical ABAP only where absolutely necessary, you’ll keep your SAP environment healthy, upgrade-friendly, and truly valuable.

What’s your experience?How does your organization approach SAP customization? Share your insights or challenges in the comments or reach out directly—I would love to learn from your journey and collaborate on reducing complexity while maximizing SAP’s full potential.

 

SAP S/4HANA SAP Build SAP BTP, ABAP environment
